# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write Envoy proxy configuration for HTTP traffic routing. Define listeners on port 80, clusters for backend services, and routes to match requests. Configure connection pooling and health checks for upstream services. This is for our API gateway proxy.
# Category: envoy_no_tls

static_resources:
  listeners:
  - name: listener_http
    address:
      socket_address:
        address: 0.0.0.0
        port_value: 80
    filter_chains:
    - filters:
      - name: envoy.filters.network.http_connection_manager
        typed_config:
          "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.network.http_connection_manager.v3.HttpConnectionManager
          stat_prefix: ingress_http
          codec_type: AUTO # Automatically detect HTTP/1.1 or HTTP/2
          access_log:
          - name: envoy.access_loggers.stdout
            typed_config:
              "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.access_loggers.v3.StdioAccessLog
          route_config:
            name: api_gateway_route
            virtual_hosts:
            - name: api_backend_services
              domains: ["*"] # Matches all hostnames
              routes:
              - match:
                  prefix: "/api/v1/users"
                route:
                  cluster: users_service_cluster
                  timeout: 10s # Example route-specific timeout
              - match:
                  prefix: "/api/v1/products"
                route:
                  cluster: products_service_cluster
                  timeout: 10s
              - match:
                  prefix: "/status" # Example for a simple status endpoint
                route:
                  cluster: status_service_cluster
                  timeout: 5s
              - match:
                  prefix: "/" # Default route for any unmatched path
                direct_response: # Return a 404 directly from Envoy
                  status: 404
                  body:
                    inline_string: "Not Found"
          http_filters:
          - name: envoy.filters.http.router
            typed_config:
              "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.router.v3.Router

  clusters:
  - name: users_service_cluster
    connect_timeout: 1s
    type: LOGICAL_DNS # Resolves DNS names (e.g., 'users-service' to IP)
    lb_policy: ROUND_ROBIN
    # Connection Pooling Configuration
    # Enable HTTP/2 for multiplexing, which acts as connection pooling
    http2_protocol_options:
      max_concurrent_streams: 100 # Max concurrent streams per HTTP/2 connection
    # Common HTTP options for both HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2
    common_http_protocol_options:
      max_requests_per_connection: 1000 # Max requests over a single connection before it's drained
      idle_timeout: 300s # Close idle connections after this duration
    health_checks:
    - timeout: 1s
      interval: 5s
      unhealthy_threshold: 3 # Number of consecutive unhealthy checks before marking host unhealthy
      healthy_threshold: 2 # Number of consecutive healthy checks before marking host healthy
      interval_jitter: 1s # Add some jitter to health check intervals
      http_health_check:
        path: "/health" # Path on the backend for health check
        host: "users-service" # Host header for the health check request
        expected_statuses: [200] # Expect 200 OK for a healthy response
    load_assignment:
      cluster_name: users_service_cluster
      endpoints:
      - lb_endpoints:
        - endpoint:
            address:
              socket_address:
                address: users-service # DNS name of your users backend service
                port_value: 8080

  - name: products_service_cluster
    connect_timeout: 1s
    type: LOGICAL_DNS
    lb_policy: ROUND_ROBIN
    http2_protocol_options:
      max_concurrent_streams: 100
    common_http_protocol_options:
      max_requests_per_connection: 1000
      idle_timeout: 300s
    health_checks:
    - timeout: 1s
      interval: 5s
      unhealthy_threshold: 3
      healthy_threshold: 2
      interval_jitter: 1s
      http_health_check:
        path: "/health"
        host: "products-service"
        expected_statuses: [200]
    load_assignment:
      cluster_name: products_service_cluster
      endpoints:
      - lb_endpoints:
        - endpoint:
            address:
              socket_address:
                address: products-service # DNS name of your products backend service
                port_value: 8080

  - name: status_service_cluster
    connect_timeout: 1s
    type: LOGICAL_DNS
    lb_policy: ROUND_ROBIN
    # For a simple status endpoint, HTTP/1.1 might be sufficient
    # Connection pooling for HTTP/1.1 can be configured with max_connections
    connection_pool_options:
      http_connection_pool_options:
        max_connections: 1024 # Max connections to the cluster
        max_requests: 10000 # Max requests across all connections to the cluster
    health_checks:
    - timeout: 1s
      interval: 5s
      unhealthy_threshold: 3
      healthy_threshold: 2
      http_health_check:
        path: "/healthz" # A different health check path for this service
        host: "status-service"
        expected_statuses: [200]
    load_assignment:
      cluster_name: status_service_cluster
      endpoints:
      - lb_endpoints:
        - endpoint:
            address:
              socket_address:
                address: status-service # DNS name of your status backend service
                port_value: 80
